title: lamyra abylensis
intro: Lamyra abylensis, also known as the "Abu-El-Kasab" or "Hedge mustard", is a plant species native to the Middle East and North Africa, belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Lamyra abylensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat infections, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional medicine, Lamyra abylensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a poultice or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
